setup.tip: Use claude --model claude-sonnet-4-6 — the newest default. Faster and better than Sonnet 4.5 for code tasks. Set permanently: { "model": "claude-sonnet-4-6" } in ~/.claude/settings.json
Run claude /init in any repo — Claude reads your codebase and auto-generates a CLAUDE.md tailored to your stack. Best starting point for any new project.
Add ANTHROPIC_SMALL_FAST_MODEL=claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 to settings.json — Claude uses this for background tasks like file search, saving 80% on those calls automatically.
Install the VS Code extension (claudeai.claude-code) — inline diffs, sidebar chat, right-click any file to pin it to Claude's context. Eliminates constant copy-pasting.
setup.example: # Search 'Claude Code' in VS Code marketplace

setup.tip: Set "autoCompact": true in settings.json — sessions auto-compact at 85% context, continuing seamlessly instead of failing at the limit.
Use claude --print "explain this function" | pbcopy for quick non-interactive lookups — pipe the output anywhere without starting a full session.
Configure the status line to show model + tokens + cost: "statusLine": { "show": ["model","tokens","cost"] } — you'll immediately catch runaway sessions.

practices.tip: MEMORY.md = project long-term memory. Create ~/.claude/projects/<project>/memory/MEMORY.md, organised by topic (Architecture, Key Paths, Gotchas, User Preferences). Claude reads it at every session start.

Write CLAUDE.md for YOUR stack, not generic advice. Claude already knows React — document YOUR patterns, YOUR naming conventions, YOUR non-obvious decisions. One specific line beats five generic ones.
practices.example:
## Why We Avoid useEffect
We use React Query for all async state. useEffect is banned except for DOM side-effects.

practices.tip: Never use withInternet: true on WiFi connect calls in mobile apps — Android silently falls back to cellular when it detects a captive portal, causing false-positive internet checks. Always withInternet: false.
practices.example: # In CLAUDE.md: NEVER revert withInternet: false to withInternet: true

practices.tip: Parallel agents = free speedup. When tasks are independent (test scan + security scan + docs), issue multiple Agent tool calls in one message. Claude runs them simultaneously, 3-5x faster.
Use worktree isolation (isolation: "worktree") for risky agents — each gets its own git branch. Auto-cleaned if no changes. Your main branch is always safe.

Skills > CLAUDE.md for procedures. CLAUDE.md should say WHAT your project is. Skills should say HOW to do things (deploy, PR, migrate DB). Separation of concerns saves thousands of tokens per session.
Deny list beats permission prompts. Set "deny": ["Bash(rm -rf*)", "Bash(git push --force*)"] in settings.json — dangerous commands are blocked silently without interrupting flow.

news.tip: Claude Sonnet 4.6 is the new default — set it explicitly in settings.json to guarantee you're always on the best coding model.
/fast mode on Opus 4.6 = same quality, significantly faster output. Toggle mid-session without losing context.
Auto-memory (MEMORY.md) means Claude remembers your architecture, preferences, and key files across all future sessions automatically.
Parallel sub-agents: Claude can now run multiple agents simultaneously in one response — test coverage + security scan + docs in parallel, not sequentially.
Worktree isolation gives each sub-agent its own safe git branch — auto-cleaned on no changes, never risks your working branch.
The status line shows live cost, tokens, and model after every tool call — configure it to never be surprised by a runaway session again.
Research insight: SWE-agent proves tool interface design matters as much as model choice. Give Claude lean, purposeful tools and watch reliability jump.
